prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
**"Alright—you’ve got this.**

Right now, it feels heavy because you’ve been staring at it all morning. But here’s the truth: **action kills anxiety.** The first step is always the hardest, but once you start, momentum takes over. You don’t have to do it perfectly—just *start*. Five minutes. One small piece. Break it down.

Remember: **Future You will be so glad you didn’t wait another minute.** The resistance you’re feeling? That’s just fear of discomfort—not a sign you can’t do it. You’ve handled hard things before. This is no different.

Now take a deep breath, square your shoulders, and **go show this task who’s boss.** I believe in you—now go prove yourself right."

*(And when you’re done, celebrate. Even the small wins count.)* 💪✨
